🎚️ CuraLive Testing Plan

Step-by-step guide to validate all platform features

📋 Testing Overview

This comprehensive testing plan validates all CuraLive platform features through 7 sequential tests. Each test is designed to be completed in one session and takes 30-60 minutes.

7
Test Suites
~6 hrs
Total Time
45+
Test Cases
15
Prerequisites

🎯 Test Sequence

Test 1: Audio Bridge (PSTN Dial-In)

Validate Twilio and Telnyx integration for phone dial-in functionality

45 minutes

Test 2: Webcasts (Mux Streaming)

Validate live streaming and HLS playback for webcast audiences

45 minutes

Test 3: Video Platforms (Zoom, Teams, Webex, RTMP)

Validate integration with major video conferencing platforms

60 minutes

Test 4: Shadow Page (Operator Monitoring)

Validate operator console monitoring and control interface

30 minutes

Test 5: AI Services (Transcription, Sentiment, Q&A)

Validate AI-powered features and real-time analysis

60 minutes

Test 6: Reports (Analytics & Summaries)

Validate post-event reporting and data export functionality

45 minutes

Test 7: AI Infrastructure (LLM, Compliance, Automation)

Validate advanced AI infrastructure and automation features

60 minutes

✅ How to Use This Guide

  1. Start with the Prerequisites tab and verify all 15 items are ready
  2. Complete tests sequentially, one per day if possible
  3. Follow each step carefully and note any issues found
  4. Document results for each test
  5. Move to the next test only after current test passes

✓ Prerequisites Checklist

Complete all 15 items before starting any tests. Use the checkboxes to track your progress.

0/15 items completed

Development Environment

  • Dev server is running (pnpm dev)
  • No compilation errors in console
  • Database connection verified

External Services

  • Twilio account configured
  • Telnyx account configured
  • Mux account configured
  • Recall.ai account configured
  • Ably account configured

Test Infrastructure

  • Test event created (test-event-001)
  • Test participants registered (3+)
  • OBS Studio installed
  • Test phone numbers available (2+)

Video Platforms

  • Zoom account configured
  • Microsoft Teams account configured
  • Webex account configured

🔊 Test 1: Audio Bridge (PSTN Dial-In)

Validate Twilio and Telnyx integration for phone dial-in functionality

45 minutes In Progress

Prerequisites

  • Twilio account with active phone number
  • Telnyx account with SIP configuration
  • Test event created in OCC
  • 2 test phone numbers available

Step-by-Step Instructions

Step 1: Verify Twilio Configuration

Check that Twilio credentials are properly configured

echo $TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID

Expected: Your Twilio Account SID should be displayed

Step 2: Create Test Event in OCC

Navigate to OCC and create a test event for audio bridge testing

  1. Go to http://localhost:3000/occ
  2. Click "Create New Event"
  3. Fill in event details (name, duration, platform)
  4. Select "PSTN Dial-In" as platform
  5. Save event

Step 3: Start Event and Get Dial-In Number

Start the event and retrieve the dial-in phone number

  1. Click "Start Event" in OCC
  2. Copy the displayed dial-in phone number
  3. Note the event PIN/access code

Step 4: Test Dial-In from Phone 1

Call the dial-in number from your first test phone

  1. Dial the provided phone number
  2. Enter the event PIN when prompted
  3. Verify you hear the event audio
  4. Speak a few words to test audio input

Step 5: Verify Transcription

Check that your speech is being transcribed in real-time

  1. Look at OCC transcription panel
  2. Verify your words appear in the transcript
  3. Check speaker name is correctly identified

Step 6: Test Dial-In from Phone 2

Repeat the dial-in process from a second phone

  1. Dial the same phone number from phone 2
  2. Enter the event PIN
  3. Verify both participants can hear each other
  4. Speak to test two-way audio

Step 7: Test Disconnect and Reconnect

Verify participants can disconnect and reconnect

  1. Hang up from phone 1
  2. Verify participant is removed from OCC
  3. Dial back in from phone 1
  4. Verify participant is re-added to OCC

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Both phones can dial in successfully
  • ✓ Audio is clear and two-way communication works
  • ✓ Transcription captures speech in real-time
  • ✓ Speaker identification is correct
  • ✓ Participants can disconnect and reconnect
  • ✓ OCC shows accurate participant count

Troubleshooting

Issue: Dial-in number not working

Solution: Verify Twilio credentials are set correctly. Check that the phone number is active in Twilio dashboard.

📡 Test 2: Webcasts (Mux Streaming)

Validate live streaming and HLS playback for webcast audiences

45 minutes Not Started

Note: This test requires OBS Studio to be installed and configured. See Prerequisites tab.

Prerequisites

  • Mux account with API credentials
  • OBS Studio installed and working
  • Test event created in OCC
  • Microphone and camera working

Step-by-Step Instructions

Step 1: Create Live Stream in Mux

Create a new live stream in Mux for the test event

  1. Go to Mux dashboard
  2. Click "Create Live Stream"
  3. Note the RTMP ingest URL and stream key

Step 2: Configure OBS Studio

Set up OBS to stream to Mux

  1. Open OBS Studio
  2. Go to Settings → Stream
  3. Set Service to "Custom"
  4. Paste the RTMP URL and stream key from Mux
  5. Click Apply

Step 3: Start Streaming

Begin streaming from OBS to Mux

  1. Click "Start Streaming" in OBS
  2. Verify stream status shows "Live" in Mux dashboard
  3. Check bitrate and frame rate are stable

Step 4: View Stream in OCC

Verify the stream is visible in the OCC webcast view

  1. Navigate to OCC webcast view
  2. Verify video is playing
  3. Check audio is audible

Step 5: Test Public Playback

Share the public stream URL and verify viewers can watch

  1. Get the public playback URL from Mux
  2. Open URL in a new browser window
  3. Verify video plays without authentication
  4. Check video quality and latency

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Stream starts successfully in OBS
  • ✓ Mux shows stream as "Live"
  • ✓ OCC displays the webcast video
  • ✓ Public viewers can watch the stream
  • ✓ Video quality is HD (720p or higher)
  • ✓ Audio is synchronized with video

🎥 Test 3: Video Platforms

Validate integration with major video conferencing platforms

60 minutes Not Started

Prerequisites

  • Zoom account with meeting creation ability
  • Microsoft Teams account
  • Webex account
  • Test event created in OCC

Test Suites

Test 3A: Zoom Integration

Validate CuraLive integration with Zoom meetings

  1. Create a Zoom meeting
  2. Add CuraLive bot to the meeting
  3. Verify bot joins as participant
  4. Check audio capture is working
  5. Verify transcription appears in OCC

Test 3B: Microsoft Teams Integration

Validate CuraLive integration with Teams meetings

  1. Create a Teams meeting
  2. Add CuraLive bot to the meeting
  3. Verify bot joins as participant
  4. Check audio capture is working
  5. Verify transcription appears in OCC

Test 3C: Webex Integration

Validate CuraLive integration with Webex meetings

  1. Create a Webex meeting
  2. Add CuraLive bot to the meeting
  3. Verify bot joins as participant
  4. Check audio capture is working
  5. Verify transcription appears in OCC

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Bot successfully joins all three platforms
  • ✓ Audio is captured from each platform
  • ✓ Transcription is accurate and real-time
  • ✓ Speaker identification works correctly
  • ✓ No audio feedback or echo

👁️ Test 4: Shadow Page (Operator Monitoring)

Validate operator console monitoring and control interface

30 minutes Not Started

Prerequisites

  • OCC access with operator role
  • Active event with participants
  • Real-time transcription running

Step-by-Step Instructions

Step 1: Access Shadow Page

Navigate to the shadow page monitoring interface

  1. Go to http://localhost:3000/shadow
  2. Select your test event
  3. Verify page loads without errors

Step 2: Monitor Real-Time Metrics

Verify real-time metrics are updating

  1. Check participant count updates
  2. Verify sentiment scores are updating
  3. Check transcription is flowing
  4. Monitor audio levels

Step 3: Test Controls

Verify operator controls are functional

  1. Test mute/unmute controls
  2. Test recording start/stop
  3. Test participant removal
  4. Test alert/notification sending

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Shadow page loads correctly
  • ✓ All metrics update in real-time
  • ✓ Controls are responsive
  • ✓ Changes are reflected immediately

🤖 Test 5: AI Services

Validate AI-powered features and real-time analysis

60 minutes Not Started

Prerequisites

  • Recall.ai account configured
  • Active event with participants speaking
  • Real-time transcription running

Test Suites

Test 5A: Real-Time Transcription

Validate speech-to-text accuracy and latency

  1. Have a participant speak clearly
  2. Check transcription appears within 2 seconds
  3. Verify accuracy of transcribed text
  4. Test with different accents and speaking speeds

Test 5B: Sentiment Analysis

Validate sentiment detection and scoring

  1. Have participant speak positively
  2. Check sentiment score shows positive
  3. Have participant speak negatively
  4. Check sentiment score shows negative

Test 5C: Q&A Auto-Triage

Validate automatic question categorization

  1. Submit various questions
  2. Check auto-categorization is accurate
  3. Verify priority scoring is correct

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Transcription is accurate (>95%)
  • ✓ Latency is <2 seconds
  • ✓ Sentiment detection is accurate
  • ✓ Q&A categorization is correct

📊 Test 6: Reports

Validate post-event reporting and data export functionality

45 minutes Not Started

Prerequisites

  • Completed event with full data
  • Transcription, sentiment, and Q&A data
  • Access to post-event report page

Step-by-Step Instructions

Step 1: Generate Post-Event Report

Create a comprehensive post-event report

  1. Navigate to completed event
  2. Click "Generate Report"
  3. Wait for report generation to complete
  4. Verify report loads without errors

Step 2: Verify Report Contents

Check all report sections are populated correctly

  1. Check event summary section
  2. Verify participant statistics
  3. Check sentiment timeline graph
  4. Verify key topics extracted
  5. Check Q&A summary

Step 3: Export Transcript

Test transcript export in multiple formats

  1. Export as TXT file
  2. Export as PDF document
  3. Export as CSV data
  4. Verify all exports contain correct data

Step 4: Export Participant Data

Test participant data export

  1. Export participant list as CSV
  2. Export attendance report
  3. Export engagement metrics
  4. Verify data accuracy

Success Criteria

  • ✓ Report generates successfully
  • ✓ All sections contain correct data
  • ✓ Exports work in all formats
  • ✓ Data is accurate and complete
  • ✓ Files are properly formatted

⚙️ Test 7: AI Infrastructure

Validate advanced AI infrastructure and automation features

60 minutes Not Started

Prerequisites

  • LLM integration configured
  • Compliance rules set up
  • Automation workflows defined

Test Suites

Test 7A: LLM Integration

Validate language model integration for summaries and insights

  1. Generate AI summary of event
  2. Verify summary is accurate and concise
  3. Generate key insights from transcript
  4. Verify insights are relevant

Test 7B: Compliance Monitoring

Validate compliance flag detection

  1. Review compliance flags in transcript
  2. Verify accuracy of flagged items
  3. Check compliance score calculation

Test 7C: Automation Workflows

Validate automated actions and notifications

  1. Trigger automated workflow
  2. Verify action executes correctly
  3. Check notifications are sent

Success Criteria

  • ✓ LLM generates accurate summaries
  • ✓ Compliance detection works correctly
  • ✓ Automation workflows execute as expected
  • ✓ Notifications are timely and accurate

CuraLive Testing Guide v1.0 | Last Updated: March 13, 2026

For issues or questions, contact your development team
